children.text: Now listen to how Eminem sounds when he is rapping. Does he sound radically different between the two modalities like with Iggy Azalea or our Little Mix examples from earlier? Or does he sound somewhat similar to how he was speaking in the interview with Anderson Cooper?

Eberhardt & Freeman (2015) found that the major difference between an artist like Eminem and an artist like Iggy Azalea is that the former is not altering his voice to perform a particular character: his rap persona is an extension of his relational, linguistic self. This is why Eminem is seen as adhering to the practice of authenticity in rap music, because he is coming to composition and scripting as he is and is reflecting his sociolinguistic origins in his performance. Iggy Azalea, on the other hand, relies on an enregistered model of hip-hop, a hip-hop voice if you will, that is not reflective of her own origins or background. The key difference here is that Iggy does not show up as she is; she pretends to be someone else by putting on someone else's voice.
